sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, i.q. שָׁתָה to drink (see, as to the interchange of the letters k and t, p. 720, A ). Arab. سقى; Eth. ሰቀየ፡ to drink, to irrigate.

Hiphil-

(1) to give to drink, to furnish drink; followed by two acc. of pers. and thing, Gen 19:32 24:43 Jud. 4:19 Num 5:24 Psa 60:5 Job 22:7 Jer 9:14 35:2 followed by בְּ of thing, Psa 80:6 מִן of thing (of any thing), Son 8:2. Part. מַשְׁקֶה subst., a butler, cup-bearer, Gen 40:1 41:9 but Gen 40:21 מַשְׁקֶה denotes drink (see מַשְׁקֶה p. 520, B , No. 2); and we should thus understand the words, “he restored the chief butler again עַל מַשְׁקֵהוּ to his drink,” i.e. to his butlership, and he again gave him his office of cup-bearer.

(2) to water cattle, Gen 24:46 29:2 Exo 2:16, 17 , 19 .

(3) to irrigate, to water land, Gen 2:6, 10 Psa 104:13.

Niphal, see שָׁקַע Niphal.

Pual, to be watered, moistened. Job 21:24, “the marrow of his bones is watered,” i.e. is fresh vigorous (compare Pro 3:8 15:30 17:22 ).

Derivatives, שֹׁקֶת, מַשְׁקֶה and the two following words.
